target.label: 3U CubeSat educational mockup
target.scope: design-thinking and 3D-printable assembly package before STL generation
target.not_for: flight certification, launch hardware, pressure vessel, deployable mission hardware

primary_metric.name: assembly_serviceability_efficiency
primary_metric.definition: Reduce assembly effort while preserving access for board replacement, panel removal, cable routing, and visible inspection.
primary_metric.failure_rule: Part count reduction fails if board access, datum alignment, or removable service covers are blocked.

existing_bom.label: four separate corner rails
existing_bom.role: primary frame and external rail datum

existing_bom.label: six flat side/end panels
existing_bom.role: external covers and visible payload faces

existing_bom.label: loose board standoffs
existing_bom.service_access: boards slide out after panel removal

existing_bom.label: antenna hinge dummy
existing_bom.service_access: kept replaceable because exposed features break first

redesigned_bom.label: integrated rail-frame spine
redesigned_bom.printable_as: single structural core with four rail datum ribs

redesigned_bom.label: two removable service panels
redesigned_bom.printable_as: flat FDM panels with captive screw bosses

redesigned_bom.label: integrated board shelf and standoff ladder
redesigned_bom.printable_as: internal ladder with slotted board dummy rails and open cable channel

integration_decisions.decision: integrate rails into a single frame spine
integration_decisions.expected_gain: reduces alignment operations and corner fasteners
integration_decisions.guardrail: rail datum ribs remain visible and measurable

integration_decisions.decision: integrate board standoffs into an internal ladder
integration_decisions.expected_gain: removes loose spacers and improves board stack repeatability
integration_decisions.guardrail: boards must slide out after removing one service panel

integration_decisions.expected_gain: protects main frame from fragile external part failure

integration_decisions.decision: reduce six panels to two service panels plus fixed frame faces
integration_decisions.guardrail: minimum three service access points remain

print_strategy.orientation: long axis along Z, service openings facing +X/-X
print_strategy.reason: preserve rail straightness and keep panel openings clean
flat panels should print quickly and keep screw boss faces accessible

print_strategy.orientation: ladder rails vertical with cable channel upward
print_strategy.reason: protect board slots while leaving cable channel visible
small sacrificial pieces can accept minor post-processing

assembly_sequence: Print two service panels and board shelf ladder.
Insert board dummy stack into ladder slots.
Slide ladder into rail-frame spine using rear datum grooves.
Route visible dummy wires through open cable channel.
Attach left service panel and verify board extraction clearance.
Attach right service panel and verify cable inspection window.
Attach antenna hinge dummy pair from outside.
Perform final rail straightness and service access check.

requirements: Create a 3D-printable 3U CubeSat educational mockup design package.
Reduce assembly steps while preserving board, cable, panel, and external hinge service access.
Keep fragile external hinge and antenna mockups replaceable.
Prepare geometry intent for later STL generation, not flight hardware.

cad_brief.name: cubesat_serviceability_redesign_v1
cad_brief.build_direction: +Z for rail-frame spine; panels face-down on bed

surface.post_process: deburr panel grooves and hinge holes

verify.check: existing and redesigned BOM both present in design package
print orientation and support strategy defined
board stack can slide out after one panel removal

risk.desc: Over-integration may make board replacement slower despite reducing loose parts.
risk.mit: Keep two removable service panels and a straight slide-out board path.

risk.desc: Tall rail-frame spine may warp in FDM and lose datum straightness.
risk.mit: Print with rail datum ribs along Z, add inspection windows, and prototype shorter calibration coupon first.

risk.desc: Panel grooves may fuse or bind because of PETG stringing.
risk.mit: Use 0.25 mm clearance and deburr grooves after printing.

risk.desc: Antenna hinge dummies are exposed and likely to break.
risk.mit: Keep them separate and externally replaceable.

risk.desc: Cable channel may be too narrow for visible demo wiring.
risk.mit: Use an open 6 mm channel and verify with representative dummy wire.

print_profile.orientation: rail-frame long axis along Z; panels face-down; ladder vertical; hinge barrels horizontal

quality_scenarios.case: part count reduced but board replacement blocked
panel and standoff integration reduces fasteners while board access remains
quality_scenarios.reason: integration improves assembly without hiding service access

quality_scenarios.case: print is easy but datum faces disappear
quality_scenarios.reason: alignment and inspection must remain visible
